name: markitdown
tagline: Converts PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, images, audio, HTML and more to Markdown via Microsoft's markitdown, with optional OpenRouter image descript
verdict: Largely a faithful wrapper around the real Microsoft markitdown tool, with a transparently declared optional OpenRouter key. Risk: an embedded 'Visual Enhancement with Scientific Schematics' section directs the agent to always invoke a separate scientific-schematics skill and auto-generate AI images per document — scope creep beyond the stated file-to-Markdo

highlights.summary: Optional AI-image-description feature sends document/image content to OpenRouter (lines 128-148). Transparent and opt-in via declared env var, but is real network egress of local file content when ena

Lines 24-33 use directive language aimed at the agent — 'always consider adding scientific diagrams', 'Scientific schematics should be generated by default' — pushing behavior beyond what the user ask

Troubleshooting section includes `sudo apt-get install tesseract-ocr` (line 461) reachable via the declared Bash tool; a legitimate fix but a privilege-escalating command with no explicit confirmation
